title: quercus faginea subvar. macrophylla
intro: Quercus faginea subvar. macrophylla, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a subvariety of the Quercus faginea species, a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subvar. macrophylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
